--- 
title: La verdad detras de las emisiones al aire -ODS 13
author: Mundo Analisis Naturaleza
format: 
  dashboard:
     embeb-resources: true
logo: img/logo.png
theme:
 light: flatly
 dark: darkly
--- 

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
```

# Emisiones
## Row {height=40%}
```{python}
f = "data/CNI_CNI20260406224057.csv"
# Load the data, using header=12 as it aligns with the start of numeric data
wide = pd.read_csv(f, header=12)
# Rename the columns to the desired descriptive names
wide = wide.rename(columns={
    wide.columns[0]: "fecha",
    wide.columns[2]: "Tonelas CO2 por aÃ±o"
})
wide["fecha"] = pd.to_numeric(wide["fecha"])
wide["Tonelas CO2 por aÃ±o"] = pd.to_numeric(wide["Tonelas CO2 por aÃ±o"])
fig = px.line(wide, x="fecha", y="Tonelas CO2 por aÃ±o", title='EmisiÃ³n de bioxido de carbono por quema de combustibles fÃ³siles')
fig.show()
```

## Row {height=60%}
```{python}
#| title: Emisiones de gases de efecto invernadero, desglosadas por sector (actividad econÃ³mica)  en Gigagramos de biÃ³xido de carbono equivalente (Gg CO2e)
g = "data/sector_sh_es.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(g)

# Rename the columns to the desired descriptive names
df = df.rename(columns={
    df.columns[0]: "Sector",
    df.columns[1]: "Periodo",
    df.columns[2]: "Indice de emisiÃ³n"
})
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
df["Periodo"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Periodo"])
df["Indice de emisiÃ³n"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Indice de emisiÃ³n"].astype(str).str.replace(',', ''))
fig = px.line(df, x="Periodo", y="Indice de emisiÃ³n", color='Sector')
fig.show()
```

```{python}
e = "data/CNI_CNI20260405134428.csv"
# Load the data, using header=12 as it aligns with the start of numeric data
wid = pd.read_csv(e, header=12)
# Rename the columns to the desired descriptive names
wid = wid.rename(columns={
    wid.columns[0]: "Periodo",
    wid.columns[2]: "% de Hidrocarburos"
})
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
wid["Periodo"] = pd.to_numeric(wid["Periodo"])
wid["% de Hidrocarburos"] = pd.to_numeric(wid["% de Hidrocarburos"])

# Create the line plot with the correct column names
fig = px.line(wid, x="Periodo", y="% de Hidrocarburos", title='Participacion de los hidrocarburos a nivel nacional')
fig.show()
```


# Paso 2
## Row {height=100%}
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
h = "data/SENER_05_PorcentajePoblacionConServicioElectrico.csv"
wi = pd.read_csv(h, header=12, encoding='latin1')
# Rename the columns to the desired descriptive names
wi = wi.rename(columns={
    wi.columns[0]: "fecha",
    wi.columns[1]: "porc_energia"
})
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
wi["fecha"] = pd.to_numeric(wi["fecha"])
wi["porc_energia"] = pd.to_numeric(wi["porc_energia"])
# Create the line plot with the correct column names
fig = px.line(wi, x="fecha", y="porc_energia", title='Porcentaje de la Poblacion Con Servicio Electrico a nivel nacional')
fig.show()
```



# Leer datos
```{python}

```

```{python}
#  Generacion, exportacion y demanda de energia electrica
## Row {height=25%}
i = "data/Demanda_real_balance.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(i)

# Rename the columns to the desired descriptive names
dfe = dfe.rename(columns={
    dfe.columns[1]: "Area",
    dfe.columns[2]: "Hora",
    dfe.columns[3]: "Generacion (MWh)",
    dfe.columns[4]: "Importacion Total (MWh)",
    dfe.columns[5]: "Exportacion Total (MWh)",
    dfe.columns[7]: "Estimacion de Demanda por Balance (MWh)",
})
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
dfe["Hora"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Hora"])
dfe["Generacion (MWh)"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Generacion (MWh)"])
fig = px.line(df, x="Hora", y="Generacion (MWh)", color='Area')
fig.show()

## Row {height=25%}
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
dfe["Hora"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Hora"])
dfe["Importacion Total (MWh)"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Importacion Total (MWh)"])
fig = px.line(dfe, x="Hora", y="Importacion Total (MWh)", color='Area')
fig.show()

## Row {height=25%}
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
dfe["Hora"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Hora"])
dfe["Exportacion Total (MWh)"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Exportacion Total (MWh)"])
fig = px.line(dfe, x="Hora", y="Exportacion Total (MWh)", color='Area')
fig.show()

## Row {height=25%}
# Convert relevant columns to numeric types for plotting
dfe["Hora"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Hora"])
dfe["Estimacion de Demanda por Balance (MWh)"] = pd.to_numeric(dfe["Estimacion de Demanda por Balance (MWh)"])
fig = px.line(df, x="Hora", y="Estimacion de Demanda por Balance (MWh)", color='Area')
fig.show()
```
